req.title: Manager updates department
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a department through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a REST API endpoint for authenticated managers to update departments with mandatory field validation and audit logging. I selected five tests: (1) EP positive – valid manager update yields 200/201 and audit record; (2) NEG – missing mandatory field yields 400; (3) BVA – department name at exact maximum allowed length; (4) NEG – unauthenticated request yields 401; (5) DT – forbidden role (non-manager) yields 403. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down state.

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_valid_payload
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A department with known ID exists
actions: PATCH /api/departments/{id} with all mandatory fields populated and valid manager token
expected: Response body contains the updated department entity ID
Audit record exists for this update operation
trace: R-WEB-006: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_update_department_valid_payload(
    manager_auth_headers,
    existing_department,
    api_client,
    get_audit_record,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    payload = {
        "name": "Engineering Updated",
        "code": existing_department["code"],
        "manager_id": existing_department["manager_id"],
    }
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=manager_auth_headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("id") == dept_id
    audit = get_audit_record(entity="department", entity_id=dept_id, action="update")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist after successful update"

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A department with known ID exists
actions: PATCH /api/departments/{id} omitting the mandatory 'name' field with valid manager token
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'name'
trace: R-WEB-006: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_update_department_missing_mandatory_field(
    manager_auth_headers,
    existing_department,
    api_client,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    payload = {
        "code": existing_department["code"],
        "manager_id": existing_department["manager_id"],
        # 'name' intentionally omitted
    }
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=manager_auth_headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code == 400
    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in error_text, "Validation error must reference the missing 'name' field"

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_name_at_max_length
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A department with known ID exists
Maximum allowed name length is defined (assumed 100 characters)
actions: PATCH /api/departments/{id} with 'name' set to exactly 100 characters
expected: Response body contains the updated entity ID
Audit record exists for this update
trace: R-WEB-006: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 100

def test_manager_update_department_name_at_max_length(
    manager_auth_headers,
    existing_department,
    api_client,
    get_audit_record,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    max_name = "A" * MAX_NAME_LENGTH
    assert len(max_name) == MAX_NAME_LENGTH
    payload = {
        "name": max_name,
        "code": existing_department["code"],
        "manager_id": existing_department["manager_id"],
    }
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=manager_auth_headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("id") == dept_id
    audit = get_audit_record(entity="department", entity_id=dept_id, action="update")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist for max-length name update"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: A department with known ID exists
actions: PATCH /api/departments/{id} with valid payload but no Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
    existing_department,
    api_client,
    get_audit_record,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    payload = {
        "name": "Should Not Update",
        "code": existing_department["code"],
        "manager_id": existing_department["manager_id"],
    }
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        # no headers – unauthenticated
    )
    assert response.status_code == 401
    audit = get_audit_record(entity="department", entity_id=dept_id, action="update")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds the 'employee' role (not manager)
A department with known ID exists
actions: PATCH /api/departments/{id} with valid payload using an employee-role token
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_returns_403(
    employee_auth_headers,
    existing_department,
    api_client,
    get_audit_record,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    payload = {
        "name": "Unauthorized Update Attempt",
        "code": existing_department["code"],
        "manager_id": existing_department["manager_id"],
    }
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403
    audit = get_audit_record(entity="department", entity_id=dept_id, action="update")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written when role is forbidden"
